title: The Data Center Is the New Utility Bill
author: The Press
dek: AI data centers have moved from cloud strategy into utility-bill math. A new Press analysis of EIA electricity data tracks the fight over megawatts, water, local consent, and who pays when private compute demand lands on public grids.
imageAlt: Data-center cooling towers and pipes beside a desert water basin, with nearby neighborhoods and mountains in the distance.
imageAiCaption: Editorial image created for The Press; not a documentary photograph.
imageCaption: Editorial image created for The Press; not a documentary photograph.
excerpt: AI data centers have moved from software metaphor to utility-bill math. The Press compared EPRI and LBNL demand scenarios with EIA electricity-sales tables and found a scale problem that is really a cost-allocation problem.

socialRailPattern.notes: Cards are source cards with direct public links, not simulated social media. The article includes original EIA-based calculations and generated editorial art labeled as non-documentary.
